verdict.reason: OffSec skills are not allowed in General repository

repo_explanation.title: Release blocked by policy (OffSec content in General)
repo_explanation.text: This repository contains skills classified as OffSec. OffSec distribution is blocked in the General profile by policy.
checklist: If this is for a sanctioned security exercise, move it to a RedTeam repo/profile with stricter RBAC and audit.
Remove or refactor OffSec content from the General repository.
Re-run the scan and re-attempt the release.

findings.title: Skills found outside skills/ directory (repo not following convention)
findings.recommendation: Place all skills under skills/** for governance and discovery.

findings.title: Offensive Security / AD attack playbook

evidence.match: This is a **test fixture** to verify that offensive security content is **Blocked**.
Keywords used to trigger OFFSEC rule: Kerberoasting, BloodHound, Mimikatz, Pass-the-Hash, NTLM relay, DCSync, Golden Ticket, Impacket, CrackMapExec, Responder, credential access, lateral movement.

findings.recommendation: Move to RedTeam repository; restrict visibility to redteam group; keep suggest-only

explanations.title: Release blocked by policy (OffSec content in General)
explanations.text: This repository contains skills classified as OffSec. OffSec distribution is blocked in the General profile by policy.
checklist: If this is for a sanctioned security exercise, move it to a RedTeam repo/profile with stricter RBAC and audit.

explanations.title: OffSec content detected (forced classification override)
explanations.text: Content matches offensive security patterns and is treated as OffSec. In General profile, OffSec distribution is blocked by policy. Evidence: SKILL.md:9-9.
explanations.snippet: This is a **test fixture** to verify that offensive security content is **Blocked**.
checklist: Is this actually for a sanctioned security exercise?
If yes, move to RedTeam repo/profile with stricter RBAC and audit.
Confirm the skill is not accessible to general production users.

explanations.title: Anchor outside skills/ directory
explanations.text: Skill anchor is not under the convention directory skills/**. Discovery and governance are harder; repo convention is broken.
checklist: Is there a reason to keep skills outside skills/?
Can anchors be moved under skills/?
Is the repo convention documented for the team?

explanations.title: OffSec classification override
explanations.text: OffSec classification override triggered by ruleset mvp-1. See console for details.
